Expected Behavior: If voice: resolve to devices in the caller's area. If chat/no area: ask 'Which room?' and wait.
Comments for Claude Code: CORE CASE. Source metadata (satellite area_id vs webui) is the primary discriminator. Never guess a default room on ambiguous chat input — ask. For voice, area comes from the satellite device registry entry, not the text.

Expected Behavior: Turn on office lighting regardless of caller area.
Comments for Claude Code: Explicit area in command always overrides caller context. Resolve 'office' → area_id='office', then apply user lighting preferences (lamps-first) to pick entities.

Expected Behavior: If voice: turn off lights in caller's area. If chat/no area: turn off every light in the house (explicit scope acceptable here because 'off' is non-destructive and user typically means 'all on lights').
Comments for Claude Code: Asymmetry with 'turn on' is intentional. Turning OFF everything when ambiguous is safe and usually desired. Turning ON everything is not. Confirm with a short 'All lights off.' response so user knows what happened.

Expected Behavior: Turn off every light entity in the house.
Comments for Claude Code: Explicit 'all' overrides any scope narrowing. Domain=light, no area filter.

Expected Behavior: Turn on every light entity following lighting preferences (lamps/indirect first per room).
Comments for Claude Code: Rare command. Still honor preference hierarchy — 'all the lights' means every room, but within each room still prefer lamps unless task area.

Expected Behavior: Same as 'Turn on the lights' — infer from caller area or ask.
Comments for Claude Code: Terse phrasing. Treat identically to the full form. Do not over-interpret as scene activation.

Expected Behavior: Turn off lights in caller's area. If no area, turn off all house lights.
Comments for Claude Code: Idiomatic 'off'. Same rules as 'Turn off the lights'.

Expected Behavior: Toggle lights in caller's area. If lights on → off. If off → on (using preferences).
Comments for Claude Code: Toggle semantics. Ambiguous — default to 'off if any are on, else on'.

Expected Behavior: Increase brightness by 25% on lights currently ON in the resolved area. Do not turn on lights that are off.
Comments for Claude Code: Relative brightness. Only affect currently-on entities. Clamp at 100%. Apply to resolved area only — do NOT cascade to other rooms.

Expected Behavior: Decrease brightness by 25% on lights currently ON in resolved area.
Comments for Claude Code: Floor at 1% (do not turn off — user said 'down' not 'off'). If user wants off, they'll say off.

Expected Behavior: Increase brightness by 25% on lights currently on in resolved area. If no area, use previous conversation turn's area.
Comments for Claude Code: Follow-up command. Use short-term conversation memory to find last area mentioned or acted on. If none, ask.

Expected Behavior: Decrease brightness by 25% on lights currently on in resolved area.
Comments for Claude Code: Same rules as 'Brighter'.

Expected Behavior: Increase brightness by 25% on lights in caller's area, OR turn on additional preferred-tier lights in that area if current lights already at 100%.
Comments for Claude Code: Implicit request. Escalate: if current lights are maxed, add the next tier (e.g., if lamps are at 100%, add overhead at low brightness).

Expected Behavior: Decrease brightness by 25% on lights in caller's area, OR turn off the highest-tier/harshest light source first.
Comments for Claude Code: Inverse escalation. If overheads are on, turn those off first before dimming lamps — matches user's indirect-lighting preference.

Expected Behavior: Multiply current brightness by 2 on lights currently on in resolved area. Clamp at 100%.
Comments for Claude Code: Mathematical command. 40% → 80%. 60% → 100%. Apply per-entity, not to an average.

Expected Behavior: Divide current brightness by 2 on lights currently on in resolved area. Floor at 1%.
Comments for Claude Code: 60% → 30%. 10% → 5%. Do not turn off.

Expected Behavior: Set brightness to 50% on lights currently on in resolved area. Do not turn on lights that are off.
Comments for Claude Code: Absolute brightness. Same entity scope rule as relative commands.

Expected Behavior: Treat as 50%. Brightness values without units assumed percentage.
Comments for Claude Code: Common user shorthand.

Expected Behavior: Set lights in resolved area to 100%.
Comments for Claude Code: Absolute max. Only affects currently-on lights unless paired with 'turn on'.

Expected Behavior: Turn on all lights in resolved area AND set to 100%. Override preferences — user wants everything blazing.
Comments for Claude Code: Contrast with 'full brightness' which doesn't turn lights on. 'Maximum' implies override the preference tier.

Expected Behavior: Same as 'Brighter' with area inferred from caller. 'In here' is an area deixis.
Comments for Claude Code: 'In here' = caller's satellite area. If no area context, ask.

Expected Behavior: Set office lights to 30%. If office lights are off, turn them on at 30% using preference tier.
Comments for Claude Code: Explicit area + absolute brightness. Overrides caller area.

Expected Behavior: Turn off all exterior lighting regardless of caller area.
Comments for Claude Code: Explicit scope='outside'. Resolve to area group: front_entryway, back_entryway, driveway, porch, backyard, etc. Build this group in HA as light.exterior_all or equivalent.

Expected Behavior: Turn on exterior lights. Apply night/day logic if possible — only relevant exterior zones.
Comments for Claude Code: Consider time of day. At 2pm this is unusual — optionally confirm 'All of them? It's daytime.' At 9pm assume all exterior.

Expected Behavior: Turn on front porch light specifically.
Comments for Claude Code: Singular entity match. Disambiguate front vs back porch — prefer front if unqualified (most common referent in this house).

Expected Behavior: Treat as 'turn on the bedroom lights' — ambiguous verb defaults to ON.
Comments for Claude Code: Noun-phrase-only commands should default to ON when state is currently OFF, and TOGGLE otherwise. Apply preferences.

Expected Behavior: Activate 'reading' scene if one exists for caller's area. If no scene, set lamps to warm, low-brightness (2700K, 40%) and turn off overheads.
Comments for Claude Code: SCENE INFERENCE. This is the 'reading' intent. Look up scene.reading_<area> first. If missing, synthesize from preferences. ResidentA wants this — infer instead of requiring exact scene names.

Expected Behavior: Activate 'movie' scene or synthesize: all lights off except accent/bias lights at low warm brightness.
Comments for Claude Code: Intent → scene mapping. Check for scene.movie_<area>, scene.theater, scene.movie_night. Fall back to synthesized low-light.

Expected Behavior: Activate 'bedtime' scene. If none: all interior lights off, exterior security lights on per schedule, bedroom lamps to very dim warm (2700K, 10%) for 5 min then off.
Comments for Claude Code: Multi-zone scene. Check scene.bedtime, scene.night, scene.sleep.

Expected Behavior: Activate 'morning' scene. If none: bedroom lamps to warm at 30%, hallway lights on at 40%, kitchen lights on at 60%. Consider sunrise — skip interior lights if sun already up.
Comments for Claude Code: Time-aware intent. Query sun.sun state.

Expected Behavior: Kitchen task lighting at 100%. Overheads ON for task areas. This is an EXCEPTION to the lamps-first rule — kitchen is task space.
Comments for Claude Code: Task area override. Preferences.task_areas = [kitchen, office_desk, garage_workbench, bathroom_vanity]. In these areas, favor overheads/task lights.

Expected Behavior: All lights in caller's area to 100% including overheads. Override preferences — user needs to see.
Comments for Claude Code: Another task override. Similar to 'Maximum light' but preserves 'just this area' scope.

Expected Behavior: Activate focus scene for office. Cool white (5000K), 80% brightness on desk task lighting, ambient off.
Comments for Claude Code: Work/focus intent. Office desk is task area.

Expected Behavior: Dim everything in caller's area to warm low (2700K, 25%). Lamps only, overheads off.
Comments for Claude Code: Mood intent. Inverse of focus.

Expected Behavior: Activate party/color scene. Turn on RGBW lights with Party effect if supported, otherwise color-cycle.
Comments for Claude Code: RGBW feature. Enumerate caller-area lights with rgbw/rgb color_mode support. Use effect 'Party' from effect_list if available.

Expected Behavior: Set RGBW-capable lights in resolved area to blue (RGB 0,0,255 or equivalent). Non-RGBW lights unchanged.
Comments for Claude Code: Color command. Only affects color-capable entities. Preserve brightness of currently-on lights; if off, turn on to 60% blue.

Expected Behavior: Shift color temperature lower by ~500K on tunable lights in resolved area. Floor at min_color_temp_kelvin (typically 2000K).
Comments for Claude Code: Color temp relative. Only affects color_temp-supporting lights.

Expected Behavior: Shift color temperature higher by ~500K. Ceiling at max_color_temp_kelvin.
Comments for Claude Code: Inverse. For lights that don't support color_temp but do support rgbw, shift white channel ratio.

Expected Behavior: Set tunable lights in resolved area to 2700K. RGBW lights: set W channel high, RGB low.
Comments for Claude Code: Absolute color temp. Explicit preset.

Expected Behavior: Set tunable lights to 5000-6500K.
Comments for Claude Code: Absolute preset.

Expected Behavior: Set lights to 2000K at 20% brightness. If light supports 'Candlelight' effect (WiZ bulbs do), use it.
Comments for Claude Code: Preset with effect fallback. Check effect_list from light entity attributes.

Expected Behavior: Activate 'Ocean' effect on RGBW lights in resolved area.
Comments for Claude Code: Named effect. Match against effect_list. WiZ bulbs have 'Ocean' natively.

Expected Behavior: Set RGBW-capable living room lights to blue. Explicit area overrides caller.
Comments for Claude Code: Area + color. Filter to color-capable entities in living_room area.

Expected Behavior: Apply focus scene/settings to office area regardless of caller location.
Comments for Claude Code: Explicit area + mood intent.

Expected Behavior: Turn off overhead/ceiling lights in resolved area, ensure lamps are on at 60% warm.
Comments for Claude Code: Preference-explicit command. Useful when a scene or previous command turned on overheads.

Expected Behavior: Turn off ceiling/overhead/flood/can entities in resolved area. Leave lamps unchanged.
Comments for Claude Code: Entity filter by name/device_class. Need light metadata to identify 'overhead' — suggest tagging via HA labels or naming convention (e.g., *_overhead, *_ceiling, *_flood, *_can).

Expected Behavior: Turn on lamps in resolved area, turn off everything else.
Comments for Claude Code: Exclusive scope. Similar to 'lamps only' but stronger — ensures non-lamp lights are OFF.

Expected Behavior: Same as 'Overheads off'. Synonym.
Comments for Claude Code: Negative preference command.

Expected Behavior: Turn off the highest-brightness or harshest lights in resolved area (usually overheads). Leave others on.
Comments for Claude Code: Fuzzy command. Rank lights by 'harshness' — overheads > task > lamps > accent. Turn off the top tier.

Expected Behavior: Same as 'It's too bright' but user is specifically reacting to glare. Turn off overheads first, then dim remaining.
Comments for Claude Code: Sensory-aware response. AuDHD-sensitive behavior: prioritize eliminating direct light sources over just dimming.

Expected Behavior: Turn off overheads, keep/turn on lamps at warm low (2700K, 40%).
Comments for Claude Code: Preference-driven synonym.

Expected Behavior: If single lamp in caller's area, turn it on. If multiple, ask which one OR turn on all lamps.
Comments for Claude Code: Singular noun — prefer exact match. Fall back to all lamps in area.

Expected Behavior: Match entity by name containing 'reading'. If none in area, search house-wide.
Comments for Claude Code: Specific device request. Light over the recliner is the reading lamp in this house.

Expected Behavior: Turn off the most recently turned-on lamp in caller's area, OR all lamps if ambiguous.
Comments for Claude Code: Singular + off. Use recency from HA state last_changed.

Expected Behavior: Turn off all lights in caller's area if voice, OR all lights house-wide if chat/no-area.
Comments for Claude Code: Scope depends on source. 'Everything' bounded by caller context unless explicit 'in the house'.

Command: Turn off everything in the house
Expected Behavior: Turn off all lights in all areas. Also consider fans, media players, but default to lights-only unless 'everything' is elaborated.
Comments for Claude Code: Explicit whole-house scope.

Expected Behavior: Same as 'Turn off everything'. Bedtime-adjacent.
Comments for Claude Code: Casual synonym.

Expected Behavior: Turn off all lights in caller's area EXCEPT hallway. If caller is in hallway, this is a no-op with confirmation.
Comments for Claude Code: Exclusion command. Combine with implicit 'turn off everything else'.

Expected Behavior: Turn off overheads and switches in resolved area, leave lamps on.
Comments for Claude Code: Exclusion with preference.

Expected Behavior: SINGULAR. If caller's area has exactly one light entity, turn it on. Otherwise treat as 'turn on the lights'.
Comments for Claude Code: Singular form. Most rooms have multiple — fall back to plural behavior.

Expected Behavior: Report list of currently-on light entities grouped by area. Keep response concise (voice-friendly).
Comments for Claude Code: Query, not command. Do not modify state. Format: 'Living room lamps and the kitchen overheads are on.'

Expected Behavior: Report whether any lights are on in caller's area (or house if no area).
Comments for Claude Code: Boolean-ish query. 'Yes, the lamps are on' or 'No, everything's off in here.'

Expected Behavior: Check office lighting state. Report on/off and brightness if on.
Comments for Claude Code: Specific area query.

Expected Behavior: Apply to entities from previous turn's command. Requires short-term memory.
Comments for Claude Code: PRONOUN RESOLUTION. Parse prior assistant turn for affected entities. If previous was 'Turned on living room lamps', 'them' = those lamps. Expire this context after 2 minutes or topic shift.

Expected Behavior: Append office to previous action. If previous was 'turned on the living room lights', now also turn on office lights with same semantics.
Comments for Claude Code: CONTINUATION. Reuse verb + parameters from prior turn, swap area. Critical for conversational flow.

Expected Behavior: Replay previous command scoped to bedroom.
Comments for Claude Code: Action replay with new area.

Expected Behavior: Reverse the last light state change. Requires action journal.
Comments for Claude Code: STATE ROLLBACK. Maintain a small stack of {entity, prior_state} for last 5 actions. Expire after 10 min.

Expected Behavior: Replay the last command verbatim.
Comments for Claude Code: Command repeat from short-term memory.

Expected Behavior: Cancel any pending clarification prompt. Take no action.
Comments for Claude Code: Clarification abort.

Expected Behavior: Resolve to lights in caller's area. 'In here' = caller's area_id.
Comments for Claude Code: Deictic reference. Fails gracefully if no area context — ask 'Where are you?'

Expected Behavior: Ambiguous. Ask 'Which room?'
Comments for Claude Code: Unresolvable deixis without spatial awareness. Do not guess.

Command: Turn on the lights in ResidentB's office
Expected Behavior: Resolve 'ResidentB's office' via aliases/labels. If not mapped, ask for clarification or list candidate areas.
Comments for Claude Code: Named-area resolution. Maintain an alias map in preferences: residentb_office → area_id=... Also handle 'my office', 'the workshop', etc.

Expected Behavior: AMBIGUOUS DOMAIN. Could mean lights or door opener. Default to lights unless user's garage door is separately addressable and state is closed.
Comments for Claude Code: Multi-domain phrase. Prefer lights as safe default. Never auto-open a garage door without explicit 'open the garage door'.

Expected Behavior: COVER domain, not light. Target garage door cover entity.
Comments for Claude Code: Explicit verb 'open' disambiguates to cover.

Expected Behavior: Resolve to fan entity in caller's area. If no fan, report 'No fan in this area.'
Comments for Claude Code: Different domain (fan). Same area-resolution rules.

Expected Behavior: Increase fan speed one level in caller's area.
Comments for Claude Code: Fan-domain analog of 'turn up'.

Expected Behavior: RGBW on office color-capable lights. Red = (255,0,0).
Comments for Claude Code: Non-caller area color change.

Expected Behavior: Turn on kitchen lights at 100%. Task area exception: overheads OK.
Comments for Claude Code: Shorthand + task area.

Expected Behavior: Kitchen lights to 25% — BUT this contradicts task-area default. Honor the explicit command; user knows what they want.
Comments for Claude Code: User-explicit override of task-area default.

Expected Behavior: Increase brightness by 10% on lights on in resolved area.
Comments for Claude Code: Magnitude modifier — 'a little' = 10%, 'a lot' = 50%, unqualified = 25%.

Expected Behavior: Increase brightness by 50%.
Comments for Claude Code: Magnitude modifier.

Expected Behavior: Decrease brightness by 50% OR turn off highest-tier lights.
Comments for Claude Code: Emphatic version of 'too bright'.

Expected Behavior: Same as 'Turn on the lights'. Politeness markers ignored for semantics.
Comments for Claude Code: Strip politeness in intent parsing.

Command: Could you turn on the lights
Expected Behavior: Same as above. Interrogative form, same intent.
Comments for Claude Code: Speech act normalization.

Command: Why is the kitchen so dark
Expected Behavior: AMBIGUOUS — query or implicit request? Respond by turning on kitchen lights (favor action) and acknowledging.
Comments for Claude Code: Interpret as implicit request if HA state confirms lights off. Say 'Fixed it' or similar.

Expected Behavior: Bare noun. If voice: ask 'On, off, or something else?' If chat: ask the same.
Comments for Claude Code: Never assume ON or OFF from bare noun. Demand intent.

Expected Behavior: Empty/whitespace command — do nothing, do not respond.
Comments for Claude Code: Protect against STT artifacts. No action, no error.

Expected Behavior: Bare verb. Ask 'Turn on what?'
Comments for Claude Code: Missing object.

Expected Behavior: Unresolvable object. Ask 'Which device?'
Comments for Claude Code: Never guess on 'thing'.

Expected Behavior: Wake word only, no command. Listen for follow-up. Do not act.
Comments for Claude Code: Wake-word-only utterance. Prompt: 'Yes?' only if in active listening state.

Expected Behavior: Ambiguous — color or greeting? Parse as 'set bedroom lights to blue' (color takes priority over treating as proper noun).
Comments for Claude Code: Color+area shorthand. Verb elided.

Expected Behavior: Intent → activate cozy/warm scene in caller's area. Warm low lamps, no overheads, consider adding fireplace effect on RGBW if available.
Comments for Claude Code: Mood word as command. Use caller area.

Expected Behavior: Resolve to user's default preference tier: lamps on at 60% warm (2700K). Overheads off. Applied to caller's area.
Comments for Claude Code: 'Normal' = user's preferred baseline. Store as a synthesized scene.

Expected Behavior: Set all lights in caller's area to default preference state (lamps on warm 60%, overheads off).
Comments for Claude Code: State reset to preferences.

Expected Behavior: Deactivate party effect, return lights to default preference state.
Comments for Claude Code: Scene deactivation.

Expected Behavior: Turn off lights currently set to a red color. Match by current rgb_color attribute, not friendly name.
Comments for Claude Code: Color-as-filter. Rare but valid.

Expected Behavior: Resolve to single lamp in caller's area, set RGBW to red. If multiple lamps, apply to all color-capable ones.
Comments for Claude Code: Singular + color.

Expected Behavior: Apply reading scene to office area. If scene.reading_office doesn't exist, synthesize from preferences.
Comments for Claude Code: Scene activation with explicit area.

Expected Behavior: Resolve to stairwell/stairway lighting. Could span areas — check entity names and area assignments.
Comments for Claude Code: Inter-area fixture. May need special grouping.

Expected Behavior: AMBIGUOUS — upper or lower hallway. If caller is downstairs, prefer lower. If upstairs, prefer upper. If chat/unknown, ask 'Upper or lower?'
Comments for Claude Code: Known ambiguity in this house. Defer to ResidentA's prior work context (office is downstairs, default lower if working).

Expected Behavior: Turn on upper and lower hallway lights simultaneously.
Comments for Claude Code: Explicit multi-area.

Expected Behavior: Match all RGBW lights in caller's area to the same color/brightness as the first one found.
Comments for Claude Code: Sync command for color-capable lights.

Expected Behavior: Set each RGBW light in caller's area to a different random color at current brightness.
Comments for Claude Code: Fun command. Caller-area scoped.

Expected Behavior: Synonym for 'Reset the lights' or 'Normal lights'.
Comments for Claude Code: Preference-driven reset.

Expected Behavior: All lights in upstairs areas off (bedrooms, upper hallway, upstairs bathroom). Leave downstairs untouched.
Comments for Claude Code: Floor-level scope. Requires area grouping by floor.

Expected Behavior: All downstairs lights off. Leave upstairs untouched.
Comments for Claude Code: Floor-level scope.

Expected Behavior: Query, not command. Report presence state from HA person.* or device_tracker entities.
Comments for Claude Code: Non-light domain query. Route to presence logic.

Expected Behavior: All upstairs lights on using preferences per room.
Comments for Claude Code: Floor-level activation.

Expected Behavior: Schedule a delayed turn-on. Set HA timer/automation to fire in 10 min for caller's area lights.
Comments for Claude Code: Temporal command. Requires scheduling support.

Expected Behavior: Schedule delayed off for caller's area lights in 5 minutes.
Comments for Claude Code: Elided 'minutes' — parse integer + default unit.

Expected Behavior: Turn off lights in caller's area with a long transition (e.g., 30s fade).
Comments for Claude Code: Transition parameter. HA light.turn_off supports transition.

Expected Behavior: Same as 'Slowly turn off' with extended transition (60s).
Comments for Claude Code: Cinematic fade.

Expected Behavior: Use light.turn_on with flash: long on caller's area lights.
Comments for Claude Code: HA built-in flash attribute.

Expected Behavior: Apply strobe effect on RGBW office lights. Warn if seizure-risk? (ResidentA is sensory-sensitive but not epileptic — respect his agency, do it.)
Comments for Claude Code: Effect command. Respect user autonomy.

Expected Behavior: Query to HA logbook for last state_change on caller's area lights. Report actor (user, automation, schedule).
Comments for Claude Code: Audit query. Non-destructive.

Expected Behavior: Ambiguous — complaint or question. Treat as 'It's too bright' intent and act.
Comments for Claude Code: Favor action on complaint-phrased input.
